result:
I would hard-wire empathy directly into the human genome—as unquestionable and reflexive as flinching from pain, as automatic as blood clotting. Not the gentle pangs we feel now when tragedy is thrust in front of us, but a full-body metabolic necessity: when any conscious creature suffers, the signal ricochets through every nearby nervous system exactly the way a wound in your own foot feels. Your proteins would register someone else’s grief the way a bee registers the magnetic fields of Earth. Simply impossible to ignore or override, because denial itself would trigger a stubborn biochemical ache.

War would become a group autoimmune disorder. Exploitation would lodge in collective joints like gout. Every policy—“costs be damned”—would be re-written by instinct. The stock market would crash if too many workers were underpaid; the ticker would feel their hunger. And since the wiring would span species boundaries, factory farms and trawled oceans would sieze entire consumer nations with phantom chest pain.

One generation is all it would take. Children born into that circuitry would grow up baffled that history spent millennia searching for justification for kindness.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
